# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent edge computing security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.152.0/http/server.ts";
const port = 8000; // you can use any available port number, this is just a placeholder for now 
// we will assume that the user provides module paths in query parameters or environment variables as input to our server and they are validated before being loaded into modules dynamically using dynamic import() function.  
serve(async (req) => { // deno-lint-ignore no-explicit-any 
    const url = new URL(req.url);
    
    SECURE:{//deno deploy security requirement}
        if (!Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATH")) throw new Error('Module path not provided'); // check for module paths in environment variables or query parameters and validate them 
         const modPath = url.searchParams.get("module") || Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATH");// get the user-provided input from either env variable/query parameter  
    }    
        try { // deno deploy security requirement}
            if (modPath) await import(`./${modPath}.ts`); 
             else throw new Error('Module not found');          
         } catch (e: any){//deno-lint no-explicit-any, this is a placeholder for now. we will handle errors in the actual implementation}  
          return new Response(null, { status: 404 }); // if module doesnt exist then send back an error message or not found response to client    }));    
});
